question: Lethal triad of trauma:
option_b: Hypothermia acidosis coagulopathy
rationale: Lethal triad: hypothermia acidosis coagulopathy; each worsens the others.

question: Damage control resuscitation includes:
option_b: Permissive hypotension limited crystalloid early blood products
rationale: DCR: permissive hypotension limit crystalloid 1:1:1 blood products TXA.

question: Massive transfusion protocol activation criteria:
option_b: Hemorrhagic shock requiring greater than 10 units PRBC in 24 hours
rationale: MTP: anticipated need for greater than 10 units PRBC or ongoing major hemorrhage.

question: Whole blood resuscitation advantage:
option_b: Contains all components platelets clotting factors RBCs
rationale: Whole blood: all components balanced; closer to what patient is losing.

question: TXA contraindications:
option_b: Greater than 3 hours from injury known thrombotic disease
rationale: TXA: avoid if greater than 3 hours may increase mortality; contraindicated active thrombosis.

question: REBOA stands for:
option_a: Resuscitative Endovascular Balloon Occlusion of Aorta
option_b: Rapid Emergency Blood Occlusion Algorithm
rationale: REBOA: balloon catheter occludes aorta controls hemorrhage below; bridge to surgery.

question: Zone 1 REBOA placement:
option_b: Between left subclavian and celiac trunk
rationale: Zone 1: descending thoracic to celiac; for abdominal hemorrhage.

question: Zone 3 REBOA placement:
option_b: Infrarenal abdominal aorta
rationale: Zone 3: infrarenal to iliac; for pelvic hemorrhage.

question: Cerebral perfusion pressure equals:
option_b: Systolic BP minus ICP
rationale: CPP = MAP - ICP; maintain CPP greater than 60-70 mmHg.

question: Target CPP in severe TBI:
option_b: 60-70 mmHg
rationale: Target CPP 60-70 mmHg; too low causes ischemia too high may worsen edema.

question: ICP monitoring indicated for GCS:
rationale: ICP monitoring: GCS 8 or less with abnormal CT or 2 of 3: age greater than 40 posturing SBP less than 90.

question: Normal ICP is:
option_b: 5-15 mmHg
rationale: Normal ICP 5-15 mmHg; greater than 20-22 requires treatment.

question: Monro-Kellie doctrine states:
option_b: Skull is fixed; brain blood CSF must compensate
rationale: Monro-Kellie: rigid skull; increase in one component requires decrease in another.

question: Second impact syndrome:
option_b: Catastrophic brain swelling from second injury before recovery from first
rationale: Second impact: rare catastrophic cerebral edema from impact before first concussion resolved.

question: Diffuse axonal injury caused by:
option_b: Rotational acceleration/deceleration forces
rationale: DAI: shearing of axons from rotational forces; often poor outcome.

question: Coagulopathy in TBI occurs due to:
option_b: Release of brain tissue thromboplastin
rationale: TBI coagulopathy: brain tissue factor release activates coagulation then depletes factors.

question: SCIWORA stands for:
option_a: Spinal Cord Injury Without Obvious Radiographic Abnormality
option_b: Spine Compression In Whiplash Or Rear Accident
option_c: Spinal Column Intact Without Observable Resting Abnormality
rationale: SCIWORA: spinal cord injury with normal imaging; more common in children.

question: Central cord syndrome affects:
option_b: Upper extremities more than lower
rationale: Central cord: upper extremity weakness greater than lower; hyperextension injury.

question: Anterior cord syndrome spares:
option_b: Proprioception and vibration
rationale: Anterior cord: loss of motor pain temperature; proprioception vibration preserved.

question: Brown-Sequard syndrome results from:
option_b: Hemisection of cord with ipsilateral motor and contralateral sensory loss
rationale: Brown-Sequard: hemisection; ipsilateral motor proprioception loss; contralateral pain temp loss.

question: Autonomic dysreflexia occurs with injuries above:
option_b: T6
rationale: Autonomic dysreflexia: lesions above T6; noxious stimulus causes severe HTN bradycardia.

question: Autonomic dysreflexia treatment:
option_b: Sit patient up find and remove stimulus
rationale: Dysreflexia: sit up to lower BP; identify stimulus bladder full fecal impaction.

question: Methylprednisolone for spinal cord injury is:
option_b: No longer routinely recommended
rationale: Methylprednisolone: NASCIS studies controversial; not routinely recommended in most guidelines.

question: Tracheobronchial injury signs:
option_b: Massive air leak pneumomediastinum subcutaneous emphysema
rationale: Tracheobronchial injury: massive persistent air leak subcutaneous emphysema pneumomediastinum.

question: Traumatic aortic disruption survival depends on:
option_b: Intact adventitia containing hemorrhage
rationale: Aortic disruption: 80-90% die at scene; survivors have contained rupture by adventitia.

question: Chest X-ray finding suggesting aortic injury:
option_b: Narrow mediastinum
rationale: Aortic injury CXR: widened mediastinum loss of aortic knob apical cap tracheal deviation.

question: Definitive diagnosis of aortic injury:
option_b: CT angiography
rationale: Aortic injury: CT angiography gold standard; or TEE in unstable patients.

question: Pulmonary contusion worsens over:
option_b: 24-72 hours
rationale: Pulmonary contusion: worsens 24-72 hours as edema develops; may require intubation.

question: Myocardial contusion diagnosed by:
option_b: ECG changes troponin elevation echocardiography
rationale: Cardiac contusion: ECG arrhythmias troponin elevation echo wall motion abnormalities.

question: Diaphragmatic rupture more common on:
option_b: Left side
rationale: Diaphragm rupture: left more common; liver protects right; stomach/colon herniation.

question: FAST exam evaluates for:
option_b: Free fluid in peritoneum and pericardium
rationale: FAST: focused assessment sonography trauma; free fluid in Morrison RUQ splenorenal pelvic pericardial.

question: Positive FAST in unstable patient indicates:
option_b: Emergent laparotomy
rationale: Positive FAST plus hemodynamic instability equals OR for laparotomy.

question: DPL diagnostic peritoneal lavage positive if:
option_b: Greater than 10 mL blood or RBC greater than 100000 WBC greater than 500
rationale: DPL positive: 10 mL gross blood or RBC greater than 100000 WBC greater than 500 or bile food bacteria.

question: Solid organ injury grading system:
option_b: AAST grade I-V based on depth and vascular involvement
rationale: AAST grading: I-V increasing severity; guides operative vs non-operative management.

question: Non-operative management of splenic injury requires:
option_b: Trauma center capability for emergent OR and monitoring
rationale: Non-op spleen: requires ICU monitoring serial exams HCT OR capability for failure.

question: Retroperitoneal hemorrhage from pelvic fracture:
option_b: May not show on FAST can lose entire blood volume
rationale: Retroperitoneal bleed: FAST may be negative; can lose entire blood volume; pelvic binder.

question: Damage control surgery includes:
option_b: Hemorrhage control contamination control temporary closure
rationale: Damage control: stop bleeding contamination; temporary closure; ICU resuscitation; return to OR.

question: Fluid creep in burns:
option_b: Over-resuscitation exceeding Parkland leading to complications
rationale: Fluid creep: over-resuscitation causes abdominal compartment syndrome pulmonary edema.

question: Abdominal compartment syndrome in burns caused by:
option_b: Massive fluid resuscitation with edema
rationale: ACS in burns: massive resuscitation causes bowel wall edema increased abdominal pressure.

question: Escharotomy indications:
option_b: Circumferential full-thickness burn with compromised circulation or ventilation
rationale: Escharotomy: circumferential eschar restricting chest movement or limb perfusion.

question: Escharotomy performed:
option_b: Through eschar only not into subcutaneous fat
rationale: Escharotomy: incise through eschar only; should not significantly bleed.

question: Burn wound conversion:
option_b: Partial becomes full-thickness from inadequate perfusion or infection
rationale: Wound conversion: zone of stasis can become necrosis if hypoperfusion infection hypothermia.

question: Silver sulfadiazine contraindicated in:
option_b: Sulfa allergy pregnancy near term G6PD deficiency
rationale: SSD contraindicated: sulfa allergy pregnancy last month G6PD deficiency.

question: Burn center referral criteria include:
option_b: Greater than 10% TBSA inhalation electrical face hands feet genitals
rationale: Burn center: partial greater than 10% full-thickness inhalation electrical hands face feet genitals.

question: Primary blast injury mechanism:
option_b: Pressure wave affecting air-filled organs
rationale: Primary blast: pressure wave damages air-filled organs lungs ears GI.

question: Secondary blast injury from:
option_b: Flying debris shrapnel
rationale: Secondary blast: projectiles fragments shrapnel; most common cause of injury.

question: Tertiary blast injury from:
option_b: Flying debris
rationale: Tertiary: body propelled by blast wave into objects or structures.

question: Quaternary blast injury includes:
option_b: Debris
rationale: Quaternary: all other injuries burns inhalation crush psychological.

question: Blast lung presents with:
option_b: Dyspnea cough hemoptysis butterfly infiltrates
rationale: Blast lung: alveolar hemorrhage edema; dyspnea cough hemoptysis; butterfly pattern CXR.

question: Pregnant trauma patient blood volume changes:
option_b: Increased 45-50% masking blood loss
rationale: Pregnancy: blood volume increases 45-50%; may lose 30% before showing signs.

question: Fetal distress indicator:
option_b: FHR less than 110 or greater than 160 or late decelerations
rationale: Fetal distress: FHR abnormalities bradycardia tachycardia late decelerations loss of variability.

question: Kleihauer-Betke test detects:
option_b: Fetal blood in maternal circulation
rationale: KB test: detects fetal RBCs in maternal blood; guides RhoGAM dosing.

question: RhoGAM in trauma indicated for:
option_b: Rh-negative mother with potential fetal-maternal hemorrhage
rationale: RhoGAM: Rh-negative mother prevents sensitization from fetal blood exposure.

question: Pediatric trauma differences:
option_b: Larger head proportionally more head injuries; solid organs less protected
rationale: Pediatric trauma: large head more TBI; flexible ribs transmit force to organs; compensate then crash.

question: Child abuse skeletal findings:
option_b: Multiple fractures different healing stages metaphyseal corner fractures
rationale: Abuse fractures: multiple ages metaphyseal corner bucket handle posterior ribs scapula.

question: Elder trauma mortality higher due to:
option_b: Decreased reserve comorbidities anticoagulation
rationale: Elder trauma: decreased physiologic reserve comorbidities anticoagulation mask signs.

question: Anticoagulated patient reversal for warfarin:
option_b: Vitamin K FFP PCC 4-factor
rationale: Warfarin reversal: vitamin K IV plus PCC 4-factor or FFP for bleeding.

question: DOAC reversal agents:
option_b: Idarucizumab for dabigatran andexanet alfa for Xa inhibitors
rationale: DOAC reversal: idarucizumab Praxbind for dabigatran; andexanet alfa for rivaroxaban apixaban.

question: PCC 4-factor contains:
option_b: Factors II VII IX X proteins C and S
rationale: 4-factor PCC: all vitamin K dependent factors II VII IX X; some include proteins C S.

question: Obese patient trauma considerations:
option_b: Difficult airway larger drug doses specialized equipment
rationale: Obesity: difficult airway increased drug volumes equipment weight limits positioning.

question: Fat embolism syndrome:
option_b: 24-72 hours post fracture petechiae confusion hypoxia
rationale: Fat embolism: 24-72 hours after long bone pelvic fractures; petechiae neuro changes hypoxia.

question: Rhabdomyolysis complications:
option_b: Hyperkalemia AKI DIC compartment syndrome
rationale: Rhabdo complications: hyperK from cell lysis AKI from myoglobin DIC compartment syndrome.

question: Rhabdomyolysis treatment:
option_b: Aggressive crystalloid mannitol bicarbonate for alkalinization
rationale: Rhabdo: aggressive NS goal UOP 200-300 mL/hr; bicarb alkalinize urine; avoid nephrotoxins.

question: CK level indicating significant rhabdomyolysis:
option_b: Greater than 5000-10000 U/L
rationale: Rhabdo: CK greater than 5-10x normal; greater than 5000-10000 significant risk AKI.

question: Pelvic fracture classifications:
option_b: Tile APC LC VS;Young-Burgess
rationale: Pelvic fractures: APC anteroposterior compression LC lateral compression VS vertical shear.

question: APC pelvic fracture opens pelvis:
option_b: Like a book increasing volume
rationale: APC: opens pelvis anteriorly book open; increased pelvic volume hemorrhage.

question: Lateral compression pelvic fracture:
option_b: Closes pelvis compresses
rationale: LC: compresses pelvis; usually lower transfusion needs than APC.

question: Femoral neck fracture risk:
option_b: AVN avascular necrosis of femoral head
rationale: Femoral neck: high risk AVN; blood supply disrupted; surgical emergency.

question: Posterior hip dislocation position:
option_b: Extended abducted externally rotated
rationale: Posterior hip: flexed adducted internally rotated shortened; sciatic nerve at risk.

question: Anterior hip dislocation position:
option_b: Extended abducted externally rotated
rationale: Anterior hip: extended abducted externally rotated; femoral nerve vessels at risk.

question: Hard signs of vascular injury:
option_b: Pulsatile bleeding expanding hematoma absent pulses bruit thrill
rationale: Hard signs: require immediate intervention; pulsatile bleed expanding hematoma pulse deficit bruit.

question: Soft signs of vascular injury:
option_b: Proximity of wound history of bleeding diminished pulse nerve deficit
rationale: Soft signs: require further evaluation; proximity diminished pulse nerve deficit history.

question: ABI ankle brachial index normal:
rationale: Normal ABI: 0.9-1.2; less than 0.9 suggests arterial injury.

question: Reperfusion injury occurs from:
option_b: Return of blood flow releasing oxygen free radicals inflammatory mediators
rationale: Reperfusion: restoration of flow releases ROS inflammatory mediators; can worsen tissue damage.

question: Mangled extremity salvage vs amputation considers:
option_b: Limb severity score MESS ischemia time nerve injury contamination
rationale: Amputation decision: severity scores ischemia time warm greater than 6 hrs nerve status contamination.

question: Permissive hypotension target in penetrating trauma:
option_b: SBP 80-90 or palpable radial pulse
rationale: Penetrating: permissive hypotension SBP 80-90 until surgical control; reduces clot disruption.

question: Resuscitative thoracotomy indication:
option_b: Penetrating trauma with signs of life within 15 min or blunt within 10 min
rationale: ED thoracotomy: penetrating with recent signs of life; blunt shorter window lower survival.

question: Resuscitative thoracotomy goals:
option_b: Release tamponade control hemorrhage cross-clamp aorta internal cardiac massage
rationale: Thoracotomy: open pericardium control cardiac hemorrhage aortic cross-clamp direct cardiac massage.

question: Aortic cross-clamping:
option_b: Temporary to redirect blood to heart brain until OR
rationale: Aortic cross-clamp: temporary; redirects blood to coronary cerebral circulation; causes distal ischemia.

question: Balanced resuscitation means:
option_b: Minimize crystalloid use blood products in ratios approximating whole blood
rationale: Balanced: limit crystalloid early blood products 1:1:1 ratio; mimics whole blood.

question: Frostbite treatment:
option_b: Rapid rewarming in 40-42C water for 15-30 min
rationale: Frostbite: rapid rewarming 40-42C 104-108F water until thawed; extremely painful.

question: Refreezing frostbitten tissue:
option_b: Worse than delayed thawing; avoid if refreezing possible
rationale: Refreezing: causes more severe injury; delay thawing if cannot maintain thawed state.

question: Immersion foot trench foot:
option_b: Prolonged cold wet exposure without freezing
rationale: Immersion foot: prolonged wet cold exposure above freezing; different from frostbite.

question: Avalanche burial survival:
option_b: 90% if rescued within 15 min drops significantly after 30 min
rationale: Avalanche: survival drops rapidly; 90% if less than 15 min; 30% at 35 min; airway most critical.

question: Lightning strike mechanism:
option_b: Direct strike side flash ground current contact voltage upward streamer
rationale: Lightning mechanisms: direct side flash ground current contact upward streamer blunt.

question: Reverse triage in lightning strike MCI:
option_b: Treat apparently dead first they may have best survival
rationale: Lightning reverse triage: treat pulseless first; may survive with resuscitation unlike other MCI.

question: Snake envenomation treatment:
option_b: Immobilization antivenom supportive care
rationale: Snake bite: immobilize supportive care antivenom; avoid tourniquet ice incision suction.

question: Crotalidae polyvalent immune Fab CroFab for:
option_b: North American pit viper envenomation
rationale: CroFab: for rattlesnake copperhead cottonmouth; not coral snake.

question: Marine envenomation jellyfish treatment:
option_b: Vinegar for box jelly hot water for most others remove tentacles
rationale: Jellyfish: vinegar for box; hot water 45C for most; remove tentacles; avoid fresh water pressure.

question: Stingray envenomation treatment:
option_b: Hot water immersion 45C 113F
rationale: Stingray: hot water immersion heat denatures venom protein; wound care tetanus antibiotics.

question: TCCC phases:
option_b: Care under fire tactical field care tactical evacuation care
rationale: TCCC: CUF fire superiority; TFC systematic care; TACEVAC evacuation platform care.

question: Care Under Fire priorities:
option_b: Return fire move to cover control catastrophic hemorrhage
rationale: CUF: fire superiority; self and casualty to cover; tourniquet for life-threatening hemorrhage.

question: Tactical Field Care includes:
option_b: Systematic assessment MARCH airway breathing circulation
rationale: TFC: MARCH Massive hemorrhage Airway Respiration Circulation Hypothermia/Head injury.

question: Combat gauze hemostatic agent mechanism:
option_b: Kaolin activates clotting cascade
rationale: Combat gauze: kaolin impregnated activates factor XII clotting cascade.

question: Junctional hemorrhage treatment:
option_b: Junctional tourniquet wound packing hemostatic agents
rationale: Junctional: groin axilla neck; junctional tourniquets wound packing hemostatic dressings.

question: Needle decompression in tactical setting:
option_b: 14g 3.25 inch or longer may need 10g
rationale: Tactical: longer needles 3.25 inch or longer; 10g for larger patients; body armor considerations.

question: Hypothermia prevention in tactical:
option_b: Critical; active and passive warming from point of injury
rationale: Hypothermia prevention: critical; remove wet clothes insulate heat sources early.

question: TCCC TXA administration:
option_b: 1g IV/IO as soon as possible within 3 hours
rationale: TXA TCCC: 1g IV/IO ASAP for hemorrhagic shock anticipated transfusion; within 3 hours.

question: Prolonged field care:
option_b: Extended care when evacuation delayed greater than 4 hours
rationale: PFC: extended care when evacuation delayed; monitoring resuscitation antibiotics analgesia.
